creator: Spectral Galileo
executive_summary: US Navy formally blockaded the Strait of Hormuz on April 12 after US-Iran Islamabad talks collapsed (Vance walked out after 21 hours) — sending WTI crude +9% to $104.78 and triggering a MIXED risk-off regime with F&G=12 (Extreme Fear). This full 55-ticker run with 32 screened candidates delivers 3 high-conviction BUY trades (CVX, COP, GILD), 5 DCA setups (XAU, MSFT, NOW, CRM, V), and 4 ESPERAR positions. Today's organizing thesis: own energy and defensive healthcare; DCA tech at generational lows; hold bonds/cash buffer against ceasefire reversal. Historical accuracy from Apr 13 run: 87.5%.

macro_environment.summary: Hormuz blockade dominates all asset classes. WTI +9% to $105 is the single most important data point. F&G=12 is a historic contrarian accumulation signal matched only by COVID bottom and 2022 bear lows. SPY RSI=67.8 diverges from F&G=12, signaling internal dispersion: energy/defensives outperform while SaaS/growth bleeds. 10Y=4.317% with a positive spread of 72bp. Q1 earnings season begins this week.
key_factors: US Navy Hormuz blockade confirmed Apr 12 — 20% global oil supply at risk, no ceasefire visible
F&G=12 (Extreme Fear) — historic contrarian buy zone aligned with COVID bottom and 2022 bear lows
SaaS capitulation: NOW RSI=17.1, CRM RSI=22.2, SNOW RSI=16.8 — healthy companies at distressed prices
Q1 2026 earnings season opens this week: GILD (~Apr 23), MSFT (Apr 29), CVX/COP (late April)

historical_accuracy.notable: CVX top pick at $164 — Hormuz sent it +14.6% overnight. XAU, MSFT, GILD, NOW, BTC, CRM all reiterated.
historical_accuracy.market_note: US equity markets closed Sat-Sun. Only crypto and commodities moved overnight.

picks_tracking.note: Hormuz sent CVX +14.6% overnight. Top pick at $165 entry — thesis extends.
Gold +1.9%. Rate cut delay headwind offset by safe-haven demand. Thesis intact.
Market closed. Near SMA200 support. Earnings Apr 29 catalyst ahead.
Morgan Stanley PT raised to $175, Truist to $155. $5B Tubulis acquisition.
Market closed. UBS downgrade creates opportunity. Dan Ives: sell-off overdone.
BTC -3% on Hormuz risk-off. F&G=12 historically precedes 30-60d bounce.
Market closed. fwdPE=11.08 at historic trough. Dan Ives: overdone.
RSI=68.2 approaching overbought in risk-off regime. Wait for pullback to $175.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Chevron Corporation
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Hormuz blockade with WTI at $105 is the highest-conviction macro catalyst today. CVX has ~$50/bbl breakeven — $105 oil generates massive windfall cash flow. RSI=34.9 was already oversold BEFORE the blockade. Bandit Gulf discovery adds fundamental upside independent of geopolitics. Earnings in 13 days will surprise materially to the upside at $105 oil.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Gilead Sciences
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: PEG=0.387 is one of the cheapest large-cap biopharma valuations in the S&P 500. Morgan Stanley PT raised to $175, Truist to $155 within 48 hours. $5B Tubulis acquisition + Tempus AI collaboration signal management conviction. Healthcare is the textbook defensive sector in F&G=12 + Hormuz risk-off: zero oil exposure, zero AI disruption risk, earnings Apr 23 with beat_streak=5.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: ConocoPhillips
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Explicitly named in multiple analyst pieces as a top Hormuz beneficiary. Pre-market +3.88% confirms market pricing. fwdPE=11.27, PEG=1.82, FCF=10% — passes all moderate filter criteria. LNG diversification provides demand catalyst beyond crude. Energy sector +38% in Q1 2026; blockade extends the trade.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Gold (XAU/USD)
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Dual tailwind: Hormuz safe-haven + oil-driven inflation repricing. Goldman target $5,400, ANZ $5,800, Gabelli $6,000+. CB buying ~850t/year. RSI=62.4 not overbought. Gulf petrodollar sales known headwind but overwhelmed by institutional flows. XAU acts as portfolio insurance against every adverse tail.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Microsoft Corporation
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Highest-quality moderate-profile tech: fwdPE=19.69, PEG=1.20, FCF=23.6%, beat_streak=5. Copilot Code Red signals aggressive enterprise AI monetization. DCA Tramo 1 at $371, Tramo 2 at $352 pre-earnings Apr 29. Do not size up until rate picture clarifies.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Salesforce
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: fwdPE=11.08 at historic cheapness for Tier-1 SaaS with 39.2% FCF margin. Analysts maintaining $250-260 targets post-data-breach confirms market overreacted. Agentforce job cuts = efficiency, not collapse. DCA 3 tranches: $165, $150, $138.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: ServiceNow
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: RSI=17.1 with fwdPE=16.54, PEG=0.796, FCF=37.3%, revenue +20.7% YoY — premium SaaS at distressed price. UBS downgrade and earnings in 10 days justify DCA not full entry. Dan Ives: sell-off overdone. Tramo 1 at $83, Tramo 2 at $74, Tramo 3 at $65.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Bitcoin
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: F&G=12 historically precedes BTC bounces over 30-60d horizons. ETF AUM=$94.37B with net +$598M inflows despite blockade. Saylor perpetual dividend thesis removes sell pressure. 61.9% supply held at loss. Wait for $68K entry — do NOT buy at $70,646 current price.

watchlist.note: RSI=68.2 approaching overbought in risk-off Hormuz regime. Outstanding fundamentals but poor entry timing. Wait for pullback to $175. Buy immediately if Hormuz resolves.

cross_sector_insights.implication: CVX and COP are direct, immediate equity beneficiaries with overnight pre-market gains of 3-4%. WTI at $105 inflates their FCF in real-time — both were oversold entering the event, a rare alignment of fundamental tailwind and technical oversold. This theme has 1-4 week minimum duration given blockade logistics.

XAU benefits from dual tailwinds: Hormuz safe-haven flows AND oil-driven inflation repricing. Goldman $5,400, ANZ $5,800, CB buying 850t/year. Petrodollar reserve selling is the known headwind but overwhelmed by institutional flows. Prefer XAU over XAG in risk-off regime.

NOW (RSI=17.1), CRM (RSI=22.2), MSFT (RSI=39.5) are at multi-month capitulation lows — strong DCA signals on fundamentals. However $105 WTI feeds CPI which constrains the Fed: higher-for-longer rates compress growth multiples. DCA in 2-3 tranches over 3-6 weeks, do NOT convert to full buys until oil normalizes.

GILD is uncorrelated to Hormuz, oil, and rates. In F&G=12 extreme fear, money rotates to defensive healthcare. Dual analyst PT raises + $5B acquisition + earnings catalyst Apr 23 + beat_streak=5 make GILD the highest risk-adjusted setup.

cross_sector_insights.insight: BTC Contrarian F&G=12 Signal — Size Down
cross_sector_insights.implication: F&G=12 historically precedes BTC bounces over 30-60d. ETF AUM=$94.37B with net +$598M inflows. Saylor perpetual thesis removes sell pressure. But risk-off regime caps position: limit BTC to 5-8% of portfolio. Wait for $68K entry. ETH and SOL on bench.
